name: Music Taste Profile
description: What does your music taste say about you? Discover the genre and personality behind the songs you love.

rocker.title: The Rocker
rocker.description: You're drawn to raw energy, guitar riffs and music that says something. Whether it's classic rock, alternative, metal or indie — you value authenticity above everything. You don't follow trends; you set them.
characteristics: Values authenticity and rawness
Independent and self-assured
Passionate about what you believe in
Dislikes anything that feels manufactured
Loyal to artists who've earned your respect
rocker.strengths: Your strong sense of identity and dislike of pretension make you refreshingly honest. People trust your opinion because you never fake it.
rocker.challenges: You can sometimes be too quick to dismiss things as 'sellout' or 'mainstream'. Some things that are popular are genuinely good too.

soulful.title: The Soulful Feeler
soulful.description: You connect to music through emotion. R&B, soul, gospel, neo-soul — music that makes you feel things deeply is what speaks to you. You use music to process emotions and connect with others.
characteristics: Highly empathetic and emotionally intelligent
Music moves you physically and spiritually
Values lyrics that tell real stories
Deeply loyal to artists who pour themselves into their work
Uses music to navigate feelings
soulful.strengths: Your emotional depth means you form profound connections with both music and people. You pick up on what others miss.
soulful.challenges: Your sensitivity can sometimes make the world feel too loud. Building emotional resilience — while keeping your depth — is your ongoing work.

explorer.title: The Musical Explorer
explorer.description: Genre labels mean nothing to you. You love jazz one day, Afrobeats the next, then stumble onto an obscure folk artist from the 1970s and declare it life-changing. You hear music everywhere and in everything.
characteristics: Endlessly curious and open-minded
Discovers music no one else has heard of
Cannot describe their taste in one word
Loves the history and culture behind music
Always searching for the next discovery
explorer.strengths: Your broad taste and curiosity make you the best person to make a playlist for any occasion. You bridge worlds others never meet.
explorer.challenges: Your endless searching can make it hard to settle into one thing. Sometimes the best music is the one you already know and love.

popstar.title: The Pop Enthusiast
popstar.description: You love a hook, a drop and a song that lives in your head for a week. Pop, dance, EDM, hip-hop chart hits — you're here for all of it. You use music for fun, energy and social connection.
characteristics: Sociable and trend-aware
Music is for dancing and sharing
Knows every word to chart hits
Uses music to boost mood and energy
Unapologetically enjoys what's popular
popstar.strengths: Your love of music as a social experience means you bring people together. A playlist from you guarantees a great party.
popstar.challenges: Diving deeper into artists you love — their albums, B-sides and stories — will unlock a whole new layer of the music you already enjoy.

questions.question: A new song comes on in a shop. What's the first thing you do?
options: Listen for the guitar tone and whether the production is authentic
Pay attention to the lyrics and what emotion they carry
Try to work out the artist, genre and what era it sounds like
Check if it has a good beat and whether you'd add it to your playlist

questions.question: Your ideal music listening experience is:
options: Loud, full volume, feeling the bass in your chest at a live gig
Headphones in, alone, letting the music wash over you completely
Down a rabbit hole: one artist leads to another, then another
At a party or festival, dancing with people around you

questions.question: When a friend says your favourite artist has 'sold out', you:
options: Completely agree — you already stopped listening when they changed
Disagree — artists are allowed to grow and evolve emotionally
Listen to their old and new work and form a considered opinion
Don't really care as long as the new stuff is still fun

questions.question: How do you most often discover new music?
options: Word of mouth from people whose taste I trust completely
When I'm going through something and a song finds me
Deep dives into discographies, record labels and music history
Charts, Spotify Discover Weekly, TikTok, what's trending

questions.question: Your music library is best described as:
options: A carefully curated collection of albums I know front to back
A deeply personal mix of songs that mark moments in my life
Eclectic — no two consecutive artists are from the same genre
Full of playlists for every mood, activity and occasion

questions.question: At karaoke, you pick:
options: A classic rock anthem — Bohemian Rhapsody, Don't Stop Believin'
A slow soul ballad that lets you really feel every note
Something unexpected that nobody recognises but everyone loves
A guaranteed floor-filler — something everyone knows every word to

questions.question: What matters most in a great song?
options: Energy and authenticity — it needs to feel real and raw
Emotional truth — it should make you feel something deep
Originality — something you've never heard done quite that way
A great hook and production — it should be impossible not to enjoy

questions.question: Someone asks for a playlist recommendation. You:
options: Send them the five albums you think everyone should own
Ask what they're feeling and build around that emotion
Spend an hour curating the most interesting thematic journey
Share your party/workout/chill playlist — already perfect for any occasion
